final_answer:
From the search results, the minimum perigee value for the Earth-Moon distance is approximately 363,396 km. 

Now, let's calculate how many hours it would take Eliud Kipchoge to run 385,000 km at his record pace. 

Eliud Kipchoge's marathon world record pace is approximately 4:32:17 per marathon, which is about 26.75 minutes per mile. 

To convert this to hours per kilometer, we divide 26.75 minutes by 60 and then by 1000 (since there are 1000 meters in a kilometer):

26.75 / 60 / 1000 = 0.000445833 hours per kilometer

Now, we calculate the total time in hours to run 385,000 km:

385,000 km * 0.000445833 hours/km = 171.25 hours

Rounded to the nearest 1000 hours, it would take Eliud Kipchoge approximately 1700 hours to run the distance between the Earth and the Moon at its closest approach. 

Therefore, the answer is 1700.

You are a research assistant with access to web search and URL fetching tools.

Work step by step:
1. Search for relevant information
2. Fetch and read important pages in detail
3. Search again if you need more specific facts
4. Synthesize into a clear, sourced answer

When you have a complete answer, respond in plain text without calling any tool.

If Eliud Kipchoge could maintain his record-making marathon pace indefinitely, how many thousand hours would it take him to run the distance between the Earth and the Moon its closest approach? Please use the minimum perigee value on the Wikipedia page for the Moon when carrying out your calculation. Round your result to the nearest 1000 hours and do not use any comma separators if necessary.

response.content:
Title: Lunar distance - Wikipedia
URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lunar_distance
Snippet: The instantaneous Earth-Moon distance, or distance to the Moon, is the distance from the center of Earth to the center of the Moon. In contrast, the Lunar distance (LD or ), or Earth-Moon characteristic distance, is a unit of measure in astronomy. More technically, it is the semi-major axis of the geocentric lunar orbit. The average lunar distance is approximately 385,000 km (239,000 mi ...
---
Title: Lunar Perigee and Apogee - timeanddate.com
URL: https://www.timeanddate.com/astronomy/moon/lunar-perigee-apogee.html
Snippet: The difference between apogee and perigee. timeanddate.com Your April 2026 Moon guide Sky guide for April Elliptical Orbit The Moon's orbit around Earth is elliptical, with one side closer to Earth than the other. As a result, the distance between the Moon and Earth varies throughout the month and the year. On average, the distance is about 382,900 kilometers (238,000 miles) from the Moon's ...
---
Title: NASA - Eclipses and the Moon's Orbit
URL: https://eclipse.gsfc.nasa.gov/SEhelp/moonorbit.html
Snippet: 4.1 Introduction The Moon revolves around Earth in an elliptical orbit with a mean eccentricity of 0.0549. Thus, the Moon's center-to-center distance from Earth varies with mean values of 363,396 km at perigee to 405,504 km at apogee. The lunar orbital period with respect to the stars (sidereal month) is 27.32166 days (27d 07h 43m 12s).
---
Title: Why is the Earth-Moon distance not the same at each perigee/apogee?
URL: https://astronomy.stackexchange.com/questions/24677/why-is-the-earth-moon-distance-not-the-same-at-each-perigee-apogee
Snippet: The line from the Earth to the point at which the Moon reaches perigee does not point to a fixed position in space. It instead precesses with a period of about 8.85 years. This is what results in the so-called supermoons, which occur when the Moon's orbit is close to perigee when the Moon is full. Another such precession is the nodal precession.
---
Title: Moon at Perigee and Apogee: 2001 to 2100 - AstroPixels
URL: http://astropixels.com/ephemeris/moon/moonperap2001.html
Snippet: The Moon revolves around Earth in an elliptical orbit with a mean eccentricity of 0.0549. As a result, the Moon's distance from Earth (center-to-center) varies with mean values of 363,396 km at perigee (closest) to 405,504 km at apogee (most distant).
